mnemonics: Cyclic codes are POLAR: Polynomials, Order, Linear, Algorithmic, and Remainder-based.
keyPoints: A code is cyclic if any cyclic shift of a codeword is also a codeword.
Cyclic codes can be represented and manipulated using polynomials.
Encoding and syndrome calculation are efficiently performed using polynomial division and shift registers.
commonMistakes: Confusing the generator polynomial g(x) with the message polynomial m(x) during encoding.
Forgetting that the code is linear, so the sum of two codewords is also a codeword.
Incorrectly performing polynomial division (e.g., mixing up the order of terms) when calculating the remainder.
